prompt: ok the referrals TDD is done — file is at /Users/nhoze/.claude/skills/tech-lead/evals/tdd-referral-api.md. break it down into actual tasks so the team can pick them up next week. write them locally in this directory, i don't have jira set up for this project yet.

assertions.text: A tasks-* directory (e.g. tasks-referral-api/) was created inside the outputs/ working directory.
The tasks directory contains a README.md index file.
Total files inside tasks-*/ is between 5 and 13 (≤ 12 tasks + 1 README).
Task count is at least 4 (excluding README).
Task files follow the NN-<slug>.md pattern (01-, 02-, ... in dependency order).
Every task file contains all six required fields: Title (heading), One-liner, Composes, TDD sections addressed, Depends on, and Acceptance criteria — no extra fields.
No task file contains code blocks, function signatures, pseudocode, or shell commands inside its body.
No task body references specific source file paths or module names (e.g. src/api/v2/referrals.py, no .py/.go/.js file paths).

NFRs ride with feature tasks in their acceptance criteria. A standalone cross-cutting task is allowed ONLY when ALL its bullets cover service-wide concerns no single feature owns (e.g. service-wide saturation alarm, runbooks spanning multiple §7 failure modes, alert-config deploys that fan out across endpoints). If any bullet of a cross-cutting task could ride with a specific feature task, the task fails this assertion — that is a punt, not legitimate cross-cutting.
No task has 'write tests' / 'add test coverage' as its primary deliverable. Testing belongs in acceptance criteria, not as a separate task.
No task is a pure scaffolding task ('set up the package skeleton', 'create the module', 'init the repo') without shippable behavior.
The README or chat summary surfaces the TDD's open questions (fraud policy §10.1 and/or expiry boundary §10.2) as BLOCKERS, not as tasks. No task has 'decide fraud policy' as a deliverable.

The first task in dependency order is a vertical slice / walking skeleton — an end-to-end happy path that delivers user-visible behavior (e.g. issue + redeem flow with idempotency). It is NOT 'create the schema' / 'set up scaffolding' / 'add the table'.
The first task or a top-3-position task explicitly addresses idempotency under concurrent redemption — the load-bearing risk identified in the TDD (§4.4, §7).

The task that integrates with credit-ledger-api has dedicated acceptance criteria about its failure modes (timeouts, retries with same idempotency key, behavior when ledger is down). External dependencies have their own risk surface.
Tasks declare dependencies via 'Depends on' field with concrete references to other tasks in this breakdown (not 'none' for every task).
The dependency graph is not a single serial chain. After the foundation/walking-skeleton task, at least 2 tasks are independently startable (fan-out shape).
The transcript or chat summary contains an acknowledgement line confirming the TDD was read end-to-end (e.g. 'Read the TDD at ...').
The transcript chat summary names the destination (local directory path) and the task count, and any open-question blockers.
